prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `aa300dc3`.
codex_response:
Implemented `aa300dc3` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: recolor the longest contiguous black diagonal run to `8`, choosing the winning orientation between main-diagonal and anti-diagonal runs; when parallel runs tie within that orientation, the centered maximal run is selected.

I used the summary hint only as a starting point. `arc2_opus46_summary.json` was broadly right about “choose the longer diagonal orientation,” but the official examples required a correction on tie-breaking, so I documented that in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_aa300dc3/notes.md). No new reusable primitive was needed.

The generator matches the task family by producing fixed `10x10` gray-framed inputs with one connected black interior band, row-width profiles modeled on the official examples, and a few safe notches to create the same irregular cutout look. I validated the package with `5/5` official examples, built `1000` samples, verified `1000/1000` generated examples, and visually checked the preview sheet.
